name: El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo
region_intro: Jogging routes in Cornalvo traverse a landscape characterized by gently rolling hills, expansive oak and cork oak *dehesas*, and small mountain ranges like the Sierra Bermeja. The region features Mediterranean forests with holm oaks and cork oaks, providing shaded paths and varied scenery. Along rivers such as the Aljucén, riparian vegetation thrives, offering cooler, secluded running routes. Granite boulders are also a common sight, adding to the terrain's rugged beauty. Best jogging routes in Cornalvo The most popular jogging route is Cornalvo Reservoir Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Roman Cornalvo Dam and the surrounding *dehesas*. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra Bermeja — circular desde Mirandilla, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This route explores the varied terrain of the Sierra Bermeja hills. Local runners also love the El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo, a 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forests and past the reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Jogging in Cornalvo is defined by rolling hills, oak *dehesas*, and riparian areas. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths around the reservoir to more challenging trails in the hills. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Cornalvo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Cornalvo?
A: Cornalvo offers a diverse network of over 20 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. These routes traverse the region's unique landscapes, including rolling hills, oak &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, and riparian areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Cornalvo Natural Park?
A: The jogging trails in Cornalvo feature a varied topography, from gently rolling hills and expansive oak and cork oak &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; to small mountain ranges like the Sierra del Moro and Sierra Bermeja. You'll also find Mediterranean forests, granite boulders, and cooler, secluded paths along rivers like the Aljucén.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cornalvo?
A: Yes, Cornalvo offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.5 km long and provides scenic views of the Roman Cornalvo Dam. For a more challenging experience, consider the 18.3 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through Mediterranean forests.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see while running in Cornalvo?
A: Many running routes in Cornalvo pass by significant attractions. You can jog around the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which is still in use today, and enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero&lt;/a&gt; and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6515369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dirt Road with Cistus Flowers&lt;/a&gt;, especially beautiful in spring.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Cornalvo?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.5 km path that is well-suited for those looking for a less strenuous run with beautiful scenery. It's a great option for beginners or those seeking a relaxed pace.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Cornalvo?
A: Spring is an excellent time for jogging in Cornalvo, as the diverse flora, including beautiful cistus flowers and rare endemic orchids, is in bloom. The lush and wet seasons also make the Cornalvo Reservoir particularly beautiful. Autumn can also offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Sierra Bermeja mountains?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280321150/sierra-bermeja-circular-desde-mirandilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Bermeja — circular desde Mirandilla&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 11.7 km path that specifically explores the varied terrain and offers views of the Sierra Bermeja hills. It's a great way to experience the region's small mountain ranges.

Q: Where can I find a good spot for a break or picnic during a longer run?
A: For longer runs, you can find convenient spots for rest and refueling. There is a designated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6238086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area Near the Roman Bridge of Aljucén&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a pleasant setting to take a break amidst your run.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while running in Cornalvo?
A: Cornalvo Natural Park is rich in biodiversity, with over 200 vertebrate species. Joggers, especially around the Cornalvo Reservoir, might spot various aquatic birds. The park is also home to endangered species such as the Spanish imperial eagle, black vulture, and the iconic black stork, making it a rewarding experience for nature lovers.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running trails in Cornalvo?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like the 15.23 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirandilla loop from Mirandilla&lt;/a&gt; or the 18.3 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt; offer more demanding terrain and longer distances through the park's diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Cornalvo?
A: The komoot community highly values the running experience in Cornalvo. Over 200 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the tranquility of the environment, the well-defined paths, and the diverse landscapes that make for an immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Can I find information about the park's flora and fauna before my run?
A: Yes, the park's Visitor Center, located near Trujillanos, is an excellent resource. It offers valuable information on the local flora, fauna, and points of interest, including details on various routes that can be adapted for jogging.
tips: El Rugidero is a popular circular route in the Cornalvo Natural Park. Approximately 19.8 km. It is an excellent option for hiking lovers looking for a challenge and enjoying nature.
In spring the number of birds that can be observed from its viewpoints is spectacular, without counting the herds of cattle that come to drink and frolic on the shore of the reservoir.

Built around 130 AD, this dam was used to supply water to the ancient Roman city of Augusta Emerita. The reservoir and its surroundings were declared a natural park in 2004, making it an ideal destination for nature and history lovers.
